text: Such a welcoming spot. I always get the grilled chicken plate and it is exactly what I need after a long shift — lighter fare that does not weigh you down. Staff remembered my order on my third visit. Will keep coming back.
Nice neighborhood lunch spot. The roasted veggie wrap was fresh and not too heavy. Service was a little slow on a Tuesday afternoon but the food made up for it.
Great place for a quick weekday lunch. The turkey and avocado plate is my go-to. Portions are generous and the price is fair.
Maple Street Kitchen is exactly the kind of local gem Mint Hill needs. Fresh ingredients, friendly service, nothing overly rich or heavy on the menu. Love it.
I stop here almost every Saturday morning and it never disappoints. The blueberry muffins are gentle on the stomach and the staff is always so cheerful. My favorite spot to start the weekend.
Best bakery in Mint Hill by a mile. The cinnamon scones are incredible and the coffee is always fresh. Come early before they sell out.
Absolutely love this place. The almond danish is worth every penny. Friendly owner, cozy atmosphere, and always clean.
Really good baked goods. Parking can be tight on weekends but it is worth the hassle. The oat muffins are my personal favorite.

I was nervous trying Chinese food again but they have some wonderful steamed options here. The steamed fish with ginger is one of the gentler options on the menu and it is delicious. I appreciated being able to ask for sauce on the side.
Solid Chinese food close to the Mint Hill area. The wonton soup is light and well-seasoned. Service is fast and the staff is friendly. Prices are very reasonable.
Authentic flavors and very fresh ingredients. The moo goo gai pan is perfect. Great little neighborhood spot that deserves more attention.
This place is a lifesaver. The chicken and rice soup is the most comforting thing and easy on the stomach — exactly the kind of food I look for. The lentil soup is also wonderful. Small but spotless.
The best spot for lunch when you want something warm and light. The potato leek soup is amazing. Staff gives generous portions and the bread on the side is freshly baked.
Good variety of soups and the quality is consistent. Perfect for a quick healthy lunch. Wish they had more seating inside.

Green Fork is my top recommendation for anyone who wants lighter fare without sacrificing flavor. The grilled salmon bowl and the roasted beet salad are both outstanding. Easy on the stomach and truly satisfying.
Hands down the best healthy restaurant in the Matthews area. The grain bowls are filling and the dressings are not overpowering. Really clean flavors.
Great menu with lots of vegetable-forward options. The avocado toast is excellent and the staff is knowledgeable about ingredients. A bit pricey but worth it.

We come to Blowing Rock every October and this is always our first dinner stop. The grilled trout is perfectly prepared and the roasted vegetable sides are gentle options that do not overload you after a day of hiking. Cozy mountain atmosphere. Already looking forward to next year.
Perfect mountain restaurant. The service is warm and the food is comforting without being heavy. The apple cider pork chop is a seasonal highlight. Great wine list too.
Solid mountain tavern food. The menu leans toward classic comfort fare done well. The chowder is rich and warming on a cool October evening. Highly recommend for fall visits.
Incredible views and the food matches. The baked mountain trout is one of the best things I have eaten in years. Everything felt fresh and well-prepared. A must for any Blowing Rock trip.

Came here during our fall trip and were blown away. The garden patio is stunning and the grilled chicken with herb sauce is absolutely wonderful. Not overly rich — just really good, well-balanced food.
Beautiful outdoor seating area and an excellent menu for the region. The grilled vegetables are a standout. Reservations are strongly recommended on weekends.
Great little sandwich shop. The turkey and spinach on whole wheat is my lunch staple. They use fresh bread daily and you can taste the difference. Quick service for a workday.
Best sandwiches in Matthews. The roasted chicken panini is perfection. Friendly staff and always clean. Parking is easy too which is a bonus in this area.
Fresh fish and a good variety of preparation styles — I especially like that they offer baked and broiled options. The flounder almondine is a standout. Nice, relaxed atmosphere.
Genuinely one of the best seafood spots in the whole Charlotte metro. The grilled grouper is flaky and perfectly seasoned. Not heavy at all and the sides are freshly made.
The coziest coffee shop in Mint Hill. Great lattes and the almond milk option is always fresh. The pastries in the morning are a nice touch. I come here to wind down after early shifts.
Charming local coffee shop that beats any chain. The pour-over coffee is excellent and the staff takes their time to get your drink right. Lovely atmosphere on weekend mornings.

We always stop here on our October trips. The apple walnut scone is seasonal and absolutely wonderful. Everything is baked fresh and the smell when you walk in is amazing. A Blowing Rock tradition for us.
Lighter fare that doesn't weigh you down after a long shift. The grilled chicken plate is my usual.
GERD-friendly and kind staff — the steamed chicken and rice is gentle and satisfying.
Quiet booth, good chamomile and a scone, exactly what I wanted.
They remembered my order by the third visit. rotisserie chicken done right.
Grilled and steamed options that actually taste good. broiled fish special.
Patsy and I come here after church. The grilled salmon never disappoints.
Clean, calm, and the turkey and rice soup sits well with me.
